text:
Once upon a time in a cozy little town, lived a man named Harold. Harold had a lazy eye that could never be cured no matter how much he squinted or how many times he pruned his beloved rose bush. He wore round, old-fashioned spectacles that sat on the tip of his nose, the frames adorned with an array of colorful patches because there wasn't a whole lot left.

Harold's days were filled with ordinary tasks: mending socks, brewing coffee, and lovingly nurturing his geranium plants. One sunny afternoon, as he tended to his plants, he heard a faint voice singing about flowers that bloomed twice a day, once in the morning, once in the evening. It was an impossible flower, the story went, for they could only open their petals when struck by love.

Curiosity piqued, Harold stood and looked around to find the singing. There, in a distant corner of the meadow under the blossoming cherry trees, sat a
